item.title: Turkey Mulls Tapping $135 Billion Gold Reserves for Lira Defense
item.description: Turkey’s central bank is preparing an expanded toolkit to defend the lira from Iran war-related volatility that includes potentially tapping its vast gold reserves, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Americans Are Enamored With Chinese AI
item.description: Founders and academics across the US are finding uses for China’s leading open-source AI models.

item.title: Bessent’s Hopes for a 3% US Deficit Ratio Face Wartime Reality
item.description: The war in Iran and the Supreme Court’s tariff ruling have made Bessent’s deficit goal a mission impossible

item.title: US’s Mysterious $15 Billion Crypto Haul Faces Questions in Court
item.description: In December 2020, some $3.5 billion worth of Bitcoin was taken from its owner in what’s been called the biggest crypto hack ever. Almost five years later, the new holder of the hoard — by then worth over four times more — revealed itself. It was the US government.

item.title: Memory Is Wall Street’s Favorite Tech Trade as Mag 7 Disappoints
item.description: In the stock market’s volatile and uncertain start to the year, one trade has held strong: go long memory and storage.

item.title: Angola to Buy Back $1.75 Billion of Bonds, Plans Eurobond Sale
item.description: Angola is seeking to repurchase $1.75 billion of 8.25% notes due in 2028 as it prepares to sell new dollar-denominated bonds, according to a statement.

item.title: Musk’s Terafab Fever Dream Exposes Reality of the AI Chip Crunch
item.description: When Elon Musk took the stage on Saturday to unveil his plans to get into semiconductor production, he didn’t spare the superlatives.

item.title: Russia Curbs Some Nitrogen Fertilizer Exports as War Hits Supply
item.description: Russia has temporarily suspended exports of ammonium nitrate, further tightening the global supply of crop nutrients already strained by the Iran war.

item.title: Investors Doubt Trump’s Ability to Jawbone Markets to More Gains
item.description: Stocks are bouncing between gains and losses on the latest Iran headlines.

item.title: Telecom Italia CEO Says Telecom Firms Need Scale to Survive
item.description: Poste Italiane SpA’s €10.8 billion ($12.5 billion) bid for full control of Telecom Italia SpA moves forward much-needed consolidation that will strengthen Europe’s telecom sector, the phone company’s chief executive officer said.

item.title: LondonMetric and Schroder REIT Venture Eyes Landlord Picton
item.description: A venture between LondonMetric Property Plc and the Schroder Real Estate Investment Trust has submitted a proposal to acquire rival UK landlord Picton Property Income Ltd.

item.title: War Knocks Global Economy With Dual Shock to Growth and Prices
item.description: The world economy’s first signs of a synchronized shock emerged in business surveys revealing how the Iran war’s fallout is crippling growth momentum and stoking prices.

item.title: Russia’s Nornickel Taps AI to Seek New Demand for Its Palladium
item.description: Russia’s MMC Norilsk Nickel PJSC is betting on artificial intelligence as part of a $100 million push to find additional uses for palladium as electric vehicles erode the metal’s main source of demand.

item.title: ECB to Start Fresh Checks on Banks’ Exposure to Private Credit
item.description: The European Central Bank will begin a fresh round of checks on banks that it supervises as concerns intensify over loan quality in the private credit sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bessent’s Longshot Deficit Goal Undercut by War and Tariff News
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent regularly says his motivation to get “out from behind my desk” and enter public service was concern about growing US debt. Developments over the past several weeks have left those alarms ringing even louder.

item.title: France Moves to Support Farmers Hurt by Iran War Fuel Spike
item.description: France’s agriculture ministry announced measures to support farmers’ cash flow after a sharp rise in fuel and fertilizer prices driven by the Iran war.

item.title: Xiaomi Sales Grows Slowest in Years in Sign of Poor Phone Demand
item.description: Xiaomi Corp. posted its slowest quarterly growth since 2023, after strong sales of its EVs failed to make up for slumping smartphone demand.

item.title: South Africa Consumer Mood Extended Upward Trend Before Iran War
item.description: Consumer sentiment in South Africa picked up to the highest level since 2024 in the first quarter, a trend that looks set to be derailed by the Iran war.

item.title: World Economic Forum Postpones Saudi Conference Amid Iran War
item.description: The World Economic Forum is postponing an upcoming conference in Saudi Arabia, the latest example of a high-profile event affected by the Iran war.

item.title: ECB Is Asking Banks About Funding to SRT Buyers, Machado Says
item.description: The European Central Bank is probing to what extent banks are financing investments in significant risk transfers, highlighting how the region’s top regulator is growing increasingly wary about a potentially circular effect of the deals.

item.title: Finnish Jobless Rate Continues to Climb as Economy Stays Subdued
item.description: Finland posted another set of dire labor-market figures, with the European Union’s worst unemployment hitting levels previously seen during the first wave of the Covid-19 pandemic.

item.title: UK Growth Slows and Inflation Surges Amid Iran War, PMI Shows
item.description: British businesses barely grew this month as the war in Iran sapped demand and triggered the biggest increase in costs in more than three years, according to a closely watched survey.

item.title: China Says Japan Self-Defense Member Broke Into Tokyo Embassy
item.description: China’s Foreign Ministry said a man claiming to be an active officer of Japan’s Self-Defense Forces forcefully entered the Chinese embassy in Tokyo, prompting protest from the Chinese government.

item.title: From Crisis to Comeback, Greece Eyes Return to Developed Markets
item.description: When Greece’s stock market shut for five weeks in 2015 as capital controls gripped the country and its banks buckled under the debt crisis, a return to developed-market status looked remote.

item.title: US-Approved Iranian Barrels Find a Cautious Reception in India
item.description: India’s state-run refiners, eager for prompt crude supplies, are holding off purchases of US-approved Iranian oil and products as payment, shipping and insurance hurdles complicate potential transactions, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Euro-Zone Activity Gauge Hits 10-Month Low Amid Stagflation Fear
item.description: Private-sector activity in the euro area rose at the slowest pace since last May as the Iran war stokes inflation while endangering a nascent economic recovery.

item.title: Why the Iran War Rattled the UK Bond Market
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has rattled markets around the world, but the gyrations have been especially pronounced in UK bonds, which are particularly vulnerable to jitters over inflation and strained government finances.

item.title: Trump’s Delay Fails to Calm Jitters Over War as Funds Trim Risk
item.description: President Donald Trump set off a rebound across markets by delaying planned air strikes against Iran’s energy assets, but many investors say the latest unpredictability just confirms their bias to cut back risk exposure.

item.title: German Firms See Record Trade Barriers as Global Tensions Rise
item.description: German companies are facing the steepest trade hurdles in decades, driven by rising geopolitical tensions and growing protectionism, according to a survey by the country’s DIHK industry lobby.

item.title: German Private-Sector Activity Drops as Iran War Fuels Inflation
item.description: German private-sector activity declined more than anticipated as cost pressures spiked due to fighting in the Middle East.
